items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` **Triggered by:** commit 800a7825dbd3e2c8a13bd770b023b7ba13bc4d19 (PR #57522) by `@fowl2` ## What Changed PR #57522 enhanced `ClientCertificateCredential` documentation with platform certificate store support. In `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs`, two snippets were updated to use the recommended .NET 9+ API (`X509CertificateLoader`) via `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional blocks: - `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+ - `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+ However, `Update-Snippets.ps1` was not run after these test file changes, so `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` still shows the old `.NET 8` form without the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional branches. ## Gaps Found - `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` shows `new X509Certificate2(...)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+ - `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` shows `new X509Certificate2(_path)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+ - Users on .NET 9+ are directed to a deprecated API (`X509Certificate2` constructor with path) rather than the recommended `X509CertificateLoader` API - The `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateFromStore` snippet references `CertificateNotFoundException` without any explanation that this is a user-defined exception type — readers may be confused about where it comes from
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files No files need to be manually edited. The snippet content is generated automatically from `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` via `Update-Snippets.ps1`. Simply run that script to sync the markdown. However, the `CreateFromStore` snippet gap requires a manual prose change in the markdown. **File to edit:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` In the "Loading certificates from an X509Store" section, after the `CreateFromStore` snippet block, add a note explaining `CertificateNotFoundException`: ```markdown > **Note:** `CertificateNotFoundException` in the example above is a user-defined exception. You can replace it with any appropriate exception type (such as `InvalidOperationException`) or define your own: > > ```csharp > public sealed class CertificateNotFoundException(string message) : Exception(message) { } > ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet content for `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` and `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` must be regenerated from the test source file. The test file already contains the correct `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` branches — just run the snippet update tooling. **Test file:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/tests/samples/CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` The relevant snippets already contain the correct code with `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` / `#else` / `#endif` blocks. **README/Samples location:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` After running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify that the following snippet placeholders in the markdown were updated to include the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` block: - `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert `` - `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential `` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` is a standalone samples doc (not the main README). Its current structure covers the required scenarios. No structural changes are needed beyond the snippet sync and `CertificateNotFoundException` clarification. The main `README.md` at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` already has all required sections. No changes needed there. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — regenerates snippet blocks in markdown from `#region Snippet:` tags in test files. After running, check the git diff on `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` to confirm the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` blocks now appear in the snippet output 4. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Identity] Docs: ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md snippets out of sync with .NET 9 API updates
